decision.archive_summary: Durable instructions from t1: after each feature change, run tests, auto-commit to GitHub, auto-deploy to local OpenClaw, restart via `openclaw gateway restart` if needed, avoid hardcoding, keep docs/tests concise and maintainable, warn if context exceeds 50%, and if user testing is needed provide brief large-text instructions. Active task context from t1/t2: do not change code; run two shortest manual cases in a new Codex thread, in fixed order, and read GC metrics after each case.
decision.reasoning_summary: The active topic is still the same manual GC test flow, so `continue` fits. Keep raw turns that the next turn depends on: t1 has the authoritative task and durable constraints, t2 narrows the active plan/order, and t5 contains the concrete current test instruction. t3 is a redundant start signal and can leave the next-turn raw prompt. t4 is the latest user turn and is implicitly kept by the runtime, so it is not listed. t1 should also be semantically pinned because it contains durable user rules and preferences that may outlive this raw block.
